title: Phone calls
canDo: I can make and handle a simple phone call.

chunks.example: Hello, can I speak to Sara?

chunks.use: Offer to pass on a message.
chunks.example: She's out. Can I take a message?

chunks.use: Say you called the wrong person.

grammar.pattern: Can I speak to...? / Can I take a message?
examples: Can I speak to the manager?

reading.passage: The phone rings. Hello, can I speak to Sara? She's not here right now. Hold on a moment, let me check. Sorry, she's out. Can I take a message? No thanks, I'll call you back later.

vocabInContext.q: 'Can I take a message?' offers to…

gist.q: Will the caller be connected straight away?

fluency432.prompt: Make a phone call asking for a person.

task.prompt: Make a call: ask for someone, then leave or take a message.

writing.model: Hello, can I speak to Sara? Hold on a moment. She's out, can I take a message? I'll call you back.

sentenceCombining.answer: She's out, so can I take a message?
I'm busy now, but I'll call you back.

task.prompt: Write a short phone conversation (3 sentences).

quiz.q: Ask to speak to someone on the phone.
Offer to pass on a message.

title: At the restaurant
canDo: I can order a meal in a restaurant.

chunks.example: Hi, a table for two, please.
Could we get the bill, please?

reading.passage: We arrive and say, a table for two, please. The waiter brings menus. What do you recommend? The fish is good, he says. I'll have the chicken, though. At the end we ask, could we get the bill? It was delicious.

vocabInContext.q: 'What do you recommend?' asks for a…

script.t: Yes, I'll have the chicken, please.

fluency432.prompt: Order a full meal at a restaurant.

task.prompt: Order a meal: get a table, ask for a recommendation, order, and pay.

writing.model: A table for two, please. What do you recommend? I'll have the chicken. Could we get the bill? It was delicious.

sentenceCombining.answer: The fish is good, but I'll have the chicken.
We've finished, so could we get the bill?

task.prompt: Write a short restaurant conversation (3 sentences).

title: Food shopping
canDo: I can talk about quantities of food.

chunks.text: How much rice do we need?
chunks.example: How much rice do we need?

chunks.use: Ask for a small countable amount.
Say you have the right amount.

grammar.pattern: how much (uncountable) / how many (countable) / some / any

reading.passage: Let's check the kitchen. How much rice do we need? There isn't any milk, and we've run out of eggs. At the shop I ask for a few apples and some bread. Is that all? Yes, that's enough.

questions.q: What have they run out of?
What does the writer buy at the shop?
choices: A few apples and some bread

script.t: No, there isn't any. We've run out.

fluency432.prompt: Check what food you need and make a shopping list aloud.

retell.prompt: Retell the kitchen check and shopping.

task.prompt: Talk about what food you have and need, with quantities.

writing.model: How much rice do we need? There isn't any milk and we've run out of eggs. A few apples, please. That's enough.

sentenceCombining.answer: There isn't any milk, and we've run out of eggs.
I have ten, so that's enough.

task.prompt: Write a short note about food you need (2-3 sentences).

quiz.q: Say a food supply is finished.
Ask for a small number of something.

title: Weekend plans
canDo: I can make suggestions and decide what to do.

chunks.example: Look at the sky, it's going to rain.

chunks.text: Why don't we watch a film?
chunks.example: Why don't we watch a film?

grammar.focus: Suggestions: let's / why don't we / shall
grammar.pattern: Let's... / Why don't we...? / What shall we...?

reading.passage: Look outside, it's going to rain. What shall we do? Let's stay in. Why don't we watch a film and order pizza? Sounds good. We can go out tomorrow if the weather is better.

questions.q: What do they decide to do?

script.t: Why don't we watch a film?

fluency432.prompt: Plan an evening with a friend, making suggestions.

retell.prompt: Retell how they decided what to do.

task.prompt: Suggest plans for tonight and agree on one.

writing.model: It's going to rain. What shall we do? Let's stay in. Why don't we watch a film? Sounds good.

sentenceCombining.answer: It's going to rain, so let's stay in.
Why don't we cook? Sounds good.

task.prompt: Write a short chat making weekend plans (3 sentences).

options: Why don't we watch a film?

title: Feelings & reactions
canDo: I can express feelings and react to others' news.

chunks.example: I'm really excited about the trip.
You can't come? That's a shame.
Cheer up, things will get better.
Well done, I'm proud of you.

reading.passage: I passed my exam, so I'm really excited! My friend failed hers. That's a shame, I told her. Don't worry, you can try again. Cheer up. When she passed the next time, I said, I'm proud of you.

questions.q: How does the writer feel about passing?
What does the writer say when the friend finally passes?

script.t: That's a shame. Don't worry, you can try again.

fluency432.prompt: React to a friend's good news and bad news.

retell.prompt: Retell the story about the exams and feelings.

task.prompt: React to a friend's news, good and bad.

writing.model: I'm really excited! You failed? That's a shame. Don't worry, you can try again. Cheer up. I'm proud of you.

sentenceCombining.answer: You passed, and I'm proud of you.

task.prompt: Write a short reply reacting to a friend's news (2-3 sentences).

quiz.q: React to a friend's bad news.

title: Telling a story
canDo: I can tell a simple story in order.

chunks.text: In the end, it was great.
chunks.example: In the end, it was great.

grammar.pattern: First... / After that... / In the end...
grammar.enhance: sequencers (first, after that, in the end)

reading.passage: Let me tell you about the trip. First, we arrived early at the lake. After that, we swam and ate by the water. There was a storm, but in the end it was great. It was such fun, and I'll never forget it.

questions.q: What did they do after arriving?
How does the writer feel about the trip?

script.t: First we swam, and in the end it was great.

fluency432.prompt: Tell the story of a recent day out, in order.

retell.prompt: Retell the trip story in order.

task.prompt: Tell a short story about a day out, using sequencing words.

writing.model: First, we arrived early. After that, we swam and ate. In the end it was great. It was such fun and I'll never forget it.

sentenceCombining.answer: First we arrived early, and after that we swam.

parts: In the end it was great.
sentenceCombining.answer: There was a storm, but in the end it was great.

task.prompt: Write a short story about a day out (3-4 sentences) using sequencing words.

options: In the end, it was great.
In the end, it was great.
